ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for creamy mushroom pasta; FOODON_00002473 (pasta dish) is the closest defensible broader class.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00GJ covers pasta dishes broadly; no specific code for creamy mushroom pasta variant exists in the published FoodEx2 catalogue.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept maps specifically to creamy mushroom pasta as a food entity.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard restaurant or home serving of creamy mushroom pasta is approximately 300 g, equivalent to a single plate portion.

nutrient_source.title: Creamy Mushroom Pasta — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Input record carries HPB provenance at confidence 0.95 for all per_100g fields. Values are accepted as sourced from HPB Foodo. Saturated fat is estimated at 3.8 g/100g based on typical cream-and-butter sauce composition cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central composite data for cream-based pasta dishes (USDA SR Legacy item 'Pasta with cream sauce'), as HPB does not separately report saturated fat for this item.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — pasta (white, boiled) analogous estimate
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. Diabetes Care 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for creamy mushroom pasta as a composite dish. GI of 49 is estimated using white pasta (boiled) as the primary carbohydrate vehicle, which has a published GI of approximately 46–52 (mean ~49) in the University of Sydney GI Database. The cream sauce, fat, and protein content are expected to further moderate glycaemic response, making 49 a conservative upper-bound estimate. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (49 × 38.4) / 100 = 18.8 for a 300 g serving (12.8 g carbohydrate per 100 g × 3).

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 196 mg/100 g; a 300 g serving delivers approximately 588 mg sodium, representing about 25% of the 2,300 mg daily limit.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to 200 g or less, avoid adding table salt or extra Parmesan, and request low-sodium preparation to keep per-meal sodium below 400 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Carbohydrate 12.8 g/100 g with low GI (~49); a 300 g serving provides approximately 38.4 g carbohydrate and a glycemic load of ~18.8.
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes can include this dish in a meal plan by limiting portions to 200 g, choosing whole-wheat pasta to increase fibre above 3 g/100 g, and pairing with a non-starchy vegetable side to blunt postprandial glucose rise.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat estimated at 3.8 g/100 g from cream and butter; a 300 g serving contributes approximately 11.4 g saturated fat, approaching the 14 g daily limit for a 2,000 kcal diet.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals managing cardiovascular disease should reduce cream quantity or substitute with evaporated skimmed milk, limit portion to 200 g, and increase mushroom proportion to lower saturated fat density while preserving beta-glucan content.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Mushrooms provide beta-glucans with immune-modulating properties; saturated fat estimated at 3.8 g/100 g is below the 5 g/100 g threshold of concern.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: This dish is generally suitable for rheumatoid arthritis; reduce cream and butter to further lower saturated fat, and use olive oil as the primary fat source to maximise MUFA and oleocanthal anti-inflammatory benefit.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Creamy mushroom pasta is a Western-style pasta dish consisting of cooked pasta — typically penne, fettuccine, or spaghetti — tossed with a rich white cream sauce made from heavy cream, butter, garlic, onion, and Parmesan cheese, combined with sautéed assorted mushrooms such as button, shiitake, or portobello. The dish has a velvety, indulgent texture with an earthy, umami-forward flavour from the mushrooms balanced by the richness of the dairy-based sauce. It is widely served in Western-style cafés and casual dining restaurants across Singapore and Malaysia as a lunch or dinner option. Nutritionally, a 100 g portion provides 135 kcal, 4.2 g protein, 7.45 g fat (estimated 3.8 g saturated), and 12.8 g carbohydrate with a low glycemic index of approximately 49. Sodium is 196 mg/100 g, making portion control important for hypertension management. The mushroom component contributes dietary fibre (1.6 g/100 g), beta-glucans, and trace minerals including calcium (34 mg) and iron (0.66 mg). The dish is vegetarian and halal-suitable. Clinically, it is a moderate-concern food for Type 2 Diabetes and cardiovascular disease due to its carbohydrate load and saturated fat content at larger portion sizes; whole-wheat pasta substitution and cream reduction are recommended modifications for health-conscious consumers.

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient and micronutrient values are sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database at confidence 0.95. Saturated fat is estimated from USDA SR Legacy cream-based pasta composite and documented as an adaptation. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database using white pasta as the primary carbohydrate vehicle, which is the standard defensible approach for composite pasta dishes.
notes: Fields set to -1 (data not available): monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g. These detailed fatty acid fractions, micronutrient, and moisture values are not separately reported in the HPB Foodo database entry for creamy mushroom pasta, and no equivalent composite entry in USDA FoodData Central provides a sufficiently close match to supply these values with confidence. Saturated fat (3.8 g/100 g) is an adapted estimate cross-referenced from USDA SR Legacy 'Pasta with cream sauce' and is documented in nutrient_source. All core macros (protein_g 4.2, fat_g 7.45, carbohydrate_g 12.8) are non-zero and sourced from HPB. GI of 49 is an analogous estimate based on white pasta (boiled) from the University of Sydney GI Database; the cream-fat-protein matrix is expected to moderate actual glycaemic response further. Glycemic load per 300 g serving = (49 × 38.4) / 100 = 18.8. This dish is classified as Western cuisine; multilingual naming requirements apply only to Singaporean, Japanese, Korean, Indonesian, Thai, and Vietnamese dishes per PROJECT_VERITAS_Ver2.md Section 7.2 — no mandatory local-language names are required for Western cuisine, and local_names fields are correctly set to null.
